Run StarCAT

Source: R/StarCAT.R
RunStarCAT.Rd

Runs starCAT on a Seurat object and appends per-cell scores and program usage to its metadata.

RunStarCAT(
  seuratObj,
  reference = "TCAT.V1",
  outputDirectory = "./starcat_output",
  assayName = "RNA",
  outputPrefix = "starcat_run",
  starcatMetadataPrefix = "starcat_",
  addUsageToMetadata = TRUE,
  cacheDirectory = NULL,
  cleanUpIntermediateFiles = TRUE
)

Arguments

seuratObj

The Seurat object containing the data to be run through starCAT.

reference

The starCAT reference to use. Either a built-in reference name (e.g. 'TCAT.V1', 'MYELOID.GLIOMA.V1', 'BONEMARROW.CD34POS.HSPC.V1') or a path to a custom reference .tsv/.txt file.

outputDirectory

The directory where starCAT outputs (<name>.rf_usage_normalized.txt and, when the reference ships add-on scores, <name>.scores.txt) will be written.

assayName

The name of the assay containing the counts matrix that will be passed to starCAT.

outputPrefix

Prefix for starCAT output files.

starcatMetadataPrefix

A prefix prepended to columns added to seuratObj@meta.data. Scores are added as <prefix>score_*, usage columns as <prefix>usage_*.

addUsageToMetadata

If TRUE, append the cNMF program usage matrix to seuratObj@meta.data. If FALSE, usage is left as a file on disk in outputDirectory.

cacheDirectory

Directory where starCAT downloads / caches built-in reference files. Defaults to <outputDirectory>/cache. Use a shared persistent path to avoid re-downloading the reference across runs.

cleanUpIntermediateFiles

If TRUE (default), delete everything RunStarCAT wrote once results are on the Seurat object. Set FALSE to keep the starCAT outputs and reference cache in outputDirectory.

Value

The input Seurat object with starCAT scores (and optionally program usage) appended to meta.data.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  seuratObj <- RunStarCAT(seuratObj = seuratObj,
                          reference = 'TCAT.V1')
} # }
